ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Internal Memorandum: MNPI controls for proposed acquisition and financing — Aurora Palm Berhad

The Company is negotiating to acquire 70% of Lapis Oils Sdn Bhd (Project Kenyalang) with an indicative purchase price range of MYR180–210 million and earn-out triggers linked to FY27 EBITDA; these terms are non-public and price-sensitive pending finalisation of the SPA.

An Islamic term financing term sheet with Cemerlang Nusantara Bank Berhad contemplates MYR220 million under Commodity Murabahah at SBR + 1.35% p.a. with a first charge over the Segamat processing plant; until definitive documentation is executed, these financing terms remain non-public.

Subtle risk: the internal slide deck labeled “Project Kenyalang v3.2” includes undisclosed capacity uplift assumptions that imply a material earnings impact; do not circulate outside wall-crossed counterparties and ensure NDA coverage.
Pursuant to CMSA s.188 and SC guidance, no insider may trade or tip others while in possession of this information; an insider list will be maintained and updated in real time.
The financing security package includes an assignment of receivables from the Batu Lintang estate and a negative pledge; these items have not been announced and must be treated as MNPI.

Issuer-relative materiality assessment: the contemplated acquisition value equals approximately 48% of audited FY25 net assets and would likely require immediate announcement and, if thresholds are met, shareholder approval in general meeting per LR Chapter 10.
no selective disclosure may occur prior to that time
no insider may trade or tip others
do not forward beyond need-to-know distribution
